title: Gastrochilus trichromus
taxonomy: Gastrochilus trichromus is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Gastrochilus. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This orchid is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Gastrochilus is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the genus, Gastrochilus trichromus is a distinct species identified by its unique characteristics.

intro: Gastrochilus trichromus, also known as the Three-coloured Ghost Orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the Himalayas. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus trichromus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat.
